title: Oxymeris regnellii
intro: Oxymeris regnellii, also known as Guaco, is a plant species native to Central and South America, belonging to the Piperaceae family. The leaves of Oxymeris regnellii have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, fever, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Guaco is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, often in combination with other plants, to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and bronchitis.
taxonomy: Oxymeris regnellii is classified within the family Mimosoideae, which is a subfamily of the legume family Fabaceae. This subfamily is known for its diverse range of species, including many species of tropical trees. Oxymeris regnellii itself is a type of tree that is native to South America, specifically in the tropical regions of Brazil and Argentina. Within the family Mimosoideae, Oxymeris regnellii belongs to the genus Oxymeris, which is a monotypic genus, meaning it contains only one species. The classification of Oxymeris regnellii reflects its unique characteristics and its distinct position within the plant kingdom.
